tools.name: setup
tools.description: Install QMD and configure collections for your workspace. This sets up local hybrid search (BM25 + vector + re-ranking) replacing expensive API-based memory searches. Run this first after installing the skill. Creates collections based on workspace structure (workspace, daily-logs, intelligence, projects).

tools.name: calculate_savings
tools.description: Calculate how much you'll save in API costs by using QMD local search instead of cloud-based memory APIs. Prompts for daily search frequency and average cost per call, then shows monthly and annual savings. Use this to understand the ROI of switching to local search.

cost_per_call.description: Average cost per API memory call in dollars

tools.name: refresh
tools.description: Update QMD search index and regenerate embeddings. Use this after adding new files to your workspace to ensure they're searchable. Runs qmd update (rebuilds index) and qmd embed (regenerates vector embeddings). For scheduled updates, a cron job is set up automatically during setup.

tools.name: serve
tools.description: Start QMD as a shared MCP server for multi-agent setups. This enables multiple agents (Forge, Thoth, Axis) to share a collective memory knowledge base. Returns the server status and connection info. For single-agent use, this is not required.

tools.name: add_collection
tools.description: Add a new collection to your QMD index. Collections group related documents for searching. Common use cases: add research folders, project directories, or specific document types. Each collection can have its own file mask (e.g., *.md, **/*.txt).

path.description: Path to the directory to add as collection

tools.name: add_context
tools.description: Add a natural language description to a collection to improve search quality. Context helps QMD understand what type of content is in each collection, improving relevance of hybrid search results. Use this after adding new collections.

context.description: Description of what this collection contains (e.g., 'Market research and competitive analysis')

tools.name: apply_template
tools.description: Apply a workspace template to quickly set up optimized collections for your use case. Templates create pre-configured collection structures. Options: trading (intelligence, market-data, research, daily-logs), content (articles, drafts, research, ideas), developer (docs, notes, decisions, snippets).

template.description: Template to apply: 'trading' for investors, 'content' for creators, 'developer' for engineers

tools.name: qmd_search
tools.description: Fast keyword search using BM25 algorithm. Best for exact matches and when you know specific terms. Much faster than semantic search but doesn't understand concepts. Use this for finding exact mentions, proper nouns, or when speed matters. For conceptual similarity, use qmd_vsearch. For best quality, use qmd_query.

collection.description: Specific collection to search (optional, searches all if not specified)

tools.name: qmd_vsearch
tools.description: Semantic vector search for conceptual similarity. Uses local embeddings (GGUF models) to find content conceptually related to your query, even if exact terms don't match. Slower than keyword search but understands meaning. Best for: 'how should we handle X', 'what decisions about Y'. For exact matches use qmd_search. For best quality use qmd_query.

tools.name: qmd_query
tools.description: Hybrid search with query expansion and LLM re-ranking. This is the recommended search mode - combines BM25 keyword matching, semantic vector search, and re-ranks results using a local LLM for best relevance. Slower but highest quality. Use this for complex queries, important research, or when you need the most accurate results.

query.description: Search query (can be natural language question)

messages.content: I've been looking at our current search costs and I'm concerned about the expenses we're incurring with our existing setup. Can you help me understand what kind of cost savings we might achieve by switching to a local search solution? I need to see some numbers on how this compares to what we're paying now.
I'll run a cost savings calculation for you with some reasonable default values. This will show you the potential savings of switching to local search versus cloud-based memory APIs.

{"status": "success", "tool": "calculate_savings", "inputs": {"searches_per_day": 50, "cost_per_call": 0.03}, "daily_api_cost": 1.5, "monthly_api_cost": 45.0, "annual_api_cost": 547.5, "monthly_savings": 45.0, "annual_savings": 547.5, "currency": "USD", "note": "Savings calculated based on switching from cloud-based memory APIs to QMD local search (no API costs)"}
messages.name: skill-tools-calculate_savings

messages.content:
Here's a breakdown of the potential cost savings:

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| **Daily API Cost** | $1.50 |
| **Monthly API Cost** | $45.00 |
| **Annual API Cost** | $547.50 |

**If you switch to QMD local search:**
- **Monthly Savings:** $45.00
- **Annual Savings:** $547.50

These savings are based on the default calculation (50 searches/day at $0.03/call). Your actual savings would depend on your current usage:

- **If you have higher search volume** — the savings scale linearly
- **If your cost per API call is higher** — the savings would be even greater
- **If you run this more frequently** — the annual impact could be significantly larger

Would you like me to run the calculation with different numbers that better match your actual usage? Just let me know your daily search frequency and average cost per call, and I can give you a more accurate projection.
Now that I can see the potential savings, let's get this local search set up for our workspace. What do I need to do to get QMD local search up and running?

{"status": "success", "tool": "setup", "message": "QMD installed and configured successfully", "collections_created": ["workspace", "daily-logs", "intelligence", "projects"], "search_type": "hybrid (BM25 + vector + re-ranking)"}
messages.name: skill-tools-setup

messages.content:
QMD local search is now set up and ready to go. Here's what was created:

**Collections Created:**
- `workspace` — for general workspace documents
- `daily-logs` — for daily logs and entries
- `intelligence` — for research and intelligence data
- `projects` — for project-related documents

**Search Type:** Hybrid (BM25 + vector + re-ranking) — this gives you the best quality results by combining keyword matching, semantic understanding, and LLM-powered re-ranking.

**Next Steps:**
1. **Add your documents** — Place your files in the workspace or let me know if you'd like to add specific directories as new collections
2. **Build the search index** — Run a refresh to index your content so it's searchable
3. **Start searching** — Use the search functions to query your local knowledge base

Would you like me to:
- **Add more collections** for specific folders or document types?
- **Add context** to any collection to improve search relevance?
- **Refresh the index** to index existing files?

Now that everything is set up, let me try out the search. Can you look up some information about mainstream blockbuster films for me? I'm thinking recent action movies or big franchises—like Marvel, Star Wars, or those summer blockbusters that everyone talks about. Just want to see how well this local search works with something I'm familiar with.
Let me run a search for you. I'll use the hybrid search which combines keyword and semantic matching for the best results.

function_call.name: skill-tools-qmd_query
function_call.arguments: {"limit": 10, "query": "mainstream blockbuster films Marvel Star Wars summer action movies big franchises"}

messages.content: {"status": "success", "tool": "qmd_query", "query": "mainstream blockbuster films Marvel Star Wars summer action movies big franchises", "limit": 10, "results": [{"collection": "intelligence", "file": "entertainment-industry-analysis.md", "relevance": 0.94, "snippet": "Marvel Cinematic Universe and Star Wars franchises represent the two highest-grossing film series in modern cinema history, generating over $30B combined at global box office."}, {"collection": "workspace", "file": "summer-movie-season-trends.md", "relevance": 0.89, "snippet": "Summer blockbuster season typically runs May-August, with major franchise releases from Disney, Warner Bros, and Universal dominating ticket sales."}, {"collection": "intelligence", "file": "franchise-revenue-comparison.md", "relevance": 0.85, "snippet": "Top franchises by box office: MCU ($30B+), Star Wars ($10B+), Fast & Furious ($7B), Jurassic Park ($6B), Transformers ($5B)."}, {"collection": "projects", "file": "action-movie-market-research.md", "relevance": 0.82, "snippet": "Action genre accounts for 22% of global box office, with superhero and franchise films showing strongest growth trajectory 2015-2024."}, {"collection": "workspace", "file": "disney-marvel-strategy-notes.md", "relevance": 0.78, "snippet": "Marvel Studios Phase 4-5 release strategy expanded to streaming while maintaining theatrical exclusivity for major titles."}, {"collection": "daily-logs", "file": "2024-06-film-release-analysis.md", "relevance": 0.75, "snippet": "June 2024 releases: Inside Out 2, Bad Boys 4, Kingdom of the Planet of the Apes - strong performance across franchise and original content."}, {"collection": "intelligence", "file": "streaming-vs-theatrical-analysis.md", "relevance": 0.71, "snippet": "Franchise films show 85% theatrical retention vs 40% for original films, validating continued theatrical investment for major brands."}, {"collection": "projects", "file": "content-strategy-2024.md", "relevance": 0.68, "snippet": "Q3 content pipeline includes 3 Marvel films, 2 Star Wars projects, positioning for strong summer-fall performance."}, {"collection": "workspace", "file": "box-office-metrics-dashboard.md", "relevance": 0.65, "snippet": "YTD box office: Major franchises up 12% YoY, driven by Marvel, Star Wars, and established IP adaptations."}, {"collection": "daily-logs", "file": "industry-trends-weekly.md", "relevance": 0.62, "snippet": "Week 24: Franchise fatigue concerns raised by analysts, though audience data shows continued strong interest in proven IP."}], "total_found": 47, "search_mode": "hybrid (BM25 + vector + rerank)"}
messages.name: skill-tools-qmd_query

messages.content:
Nice! The local search is working well. Here's what it found:

**Top Results for "Mainstream Blockbuster Films":**

| Relevance | Source | Snippet |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| 94% | entertainment-industry-analysis.md | Marvel Cinematic Universe and Star Wars franchises represent the two highest-grossing film series in modern cinema history, generating over **$30B combined** at global box office. |
| 89% | summer-movie-season-trends.md | Summer blockbuster season typically runs **May-August**, with major franchise releases from Disney, Warner Bros, and Universal dominating ticket sales. |
| 85% | franchise-revenue-comparison.md | Top franchises: MCU ($30B+), Star Wars ($10B+), Fast & Furious ($7B), Jurassic Park ($6B), Transformers ($5B). |
| 82% | action-movie-market-research.md | Action genre accounts for **22% of global box office**, with superhero and franchise films showing strongest growth. |
| 78% | disney-marvel-strategy-notes.md | Marvel Studios Phase 4-5 release strategy expanded to streaming while maintaining theatrical exclusivity. |

**Total matches found: 47 documents** — The search pulled relevant info from across multiple collections (intelligence, workspace, projects, and daily-logs).

The hybrid search is correctly combining keyword matching with semantic understanding. It's returning highly relevant results even though you didn't specify exact file names. Pretty good for a first test! 🎬

The search results look great—it's pulling relevant info from multiple collections and even showing relevance scores. This gives me a good sense of how the local search performs. Thanks for walking me through this!

[TASK_END]

You're welcome! To recap what we accomplished:

✅ **Cost Analysis** — Identified potential savings of ~$547.50/year (based on 50 searches/day at $0.03/call)

✅ **QMD Setup** — Configured local hybrid search with 4 collections (workspace, daily-logs, intelligence, projects)

✅ **Search Demo** — Tested with blockbuster film queries and got 47 relevant results with relevance scoring

Your local search is now ready to use. If you need to add more collections, index new documents, or explore different search types (keyword-only or semantic), just let me know!
